strengths: Clear local-first philosophy with optional cloud backend (OpenGravity) cleanly separates trust boundaries
Explicit safety primitives exist as dedicated modules: kill_switch (ESC monitor) and permissions (path/command guards)
Monorepo layout anticipates domain separation (coordinator, tools, bridge, db, memory, runtime, skills) per package.json
Single-executable packaging via build_sea.mjs aligns with Windows-native deployment target
Upstream dependency tracking isolated in watchers/hermes_watcher provides clean release-monitoring boundary
Pragmatic tech choices: better-sqlite3 + sqlite-vec for local vector memory avoids heavy infra
weaknesses: Security guard bypass: runner.ts uses execSync without calling permissions.isDangerousCommand(), making the safety module decorative
Planned modules (coordinator, tools, bridge, memory, skills) declared in package.json but only watchers/utils observed — architecture is aspirational, not realized
No standardized test framework despite test_b*.ts files; package.json test script is a no-op, blocking CI enforcement
Hardcoded Windows absolute paths in scripts/a4_run.ts couple integration runners to a single developer machine
Failure-masking patterns: hermes_watcher updates last_check_iso on API errors; kill_switch silently nulls proc on spawn failure
WORKSPACE_ROOT in permissions.ts falls back to cwd(), undermining the path-traversal invariant the module is meant to enforce
recommendations: Wire permissions.isDangerousCommand() into runner.ts execSync path and add a unit test asserting denial of known-bad commands
Adopt vitest (lightweight, esbuild-friendly) and convert test_b*.ts/__tests__ into a runnable suite wired to npm test and CI
Replace hardcoded paths in scripts/a4_run.ts with env vars or path.resolve relative to repo root; add a portability lint check
Fix hermes_watcher to only persist last_check_iso on successful API responses; emit structured error events on failure
Make kill_switch spawn failure fatal or surface a loud warning + retry; never silently disable the safety mechanism
Resolve license ambiguity in package.json (pick ISC or MIT) and freeze WORKSPACE_ROOT at process start from a required env var

strengths: Local-first architecture reduces cloud dependency and data exposure surface
Permission validation module (permissions.ts) exists as intentional guard layer
Kill-switch safety control (ESC monitor) demonstrates defensive design philosophy
Monorepo structure enables centralized dependency management and audit trails
Planned module separation (coordinator, tools, db, memory) suggests forward security thinking
Use of better-sqlite3 + sqlite-vec for local vector storage avoids remote ML API dependency
weaknesses: runner.ts executes commands via execSync without calling permissions.isDangerousCommand() guard; dangerous-command check exists but is bypassed, enabling arbitrary command execution
missions/ directory unreadable (90s timeouts × 2); unknown code may contain injection vulnerabilities or unsafe patterns
scripts/a4_run.ts hardcoded Windows paths (C:\Users\angel\...) enable local information disclosure and break cross-user deployments
kill_switch.ts silently fails (proc=null) if PowerShell spawn fails; safety mechanism defeated without alerting operator or admin
hermes_watcher.ts updates last_check_iso on API failures, masking outage windows and potentially delaying security update notifications
permissions.ts WORKSPACE_ROOT fallback to cwd() at runtime; path-traversal validation weakened if cwd drifts during agent execution
recommendations: Enforce permissions.isDangerousCommand() check in runner.ts execSync wrapper; block execution of risky patterns (cmd.exe /c, powershell -Command, etc.) and log all denials
Audit missions/ directory immediately with local scan or manual review; add explicit code-review gate before production deployment
Replace hardcoded paths in a4_run.ts with path.resolve(process.env.USERPROFILE, 'Documents', ...) or read from config; validate against WORKSPACE_ROOT at runtime
Modify kill_switch.ts to throw Error or emit critical event if PowerShell spawn fails; add structured logging (severity=CRITICAL) and escalate to admin notification
Update hermes_watcher.ts to only update last_check_iso on successful API responses; on failure, log error and defer state write
Pin WORKSPACE_ROOT to module load time (readFileSync or env var) instead of runtime cwd(); add assertion at agent startup to detect drift

strengths: Local-first architecture with optional cloud backend decouples dependency on external services, improving resilience for offline-first Spanish creator workflows.
Modular separation (watchers, utils, scripts, missions) with clear responsibility domains reduces tight coupling.
Custom Node SEA build (build_sea.mjs) enables single-exe distribution, lowering friction for non-technical Windows users.
TypeScript monorepo structure with consistent tooling (esbuild, tsx) simplifies dependency management and builds.
Integration test runners (a4_run.ts, b1_run.ts) demonstrate testing intent, though framework choice is weak.
Desktop automation (nut-js) + web scraping (Playwright) + vector memory (sqlite-vec) covers content-creation automation needs comprehensively.
weaknesses: Critical security gap: runner.ts executes commands via execSync without calling permissions.isDangerousCommand(), rendering the safety guard inert and exposing arbitrary code execution.
Visibility crisis: missions/, docs/, misc/ directories unreadable (subagent timeouts, validation failures); ~30% of repo structure opaque, preventing coherence assessment.
Portability broken: a4_run.ts hardcodes C:\Users\angel\... absolute paths; won't run on other machines or users, blocking collaboration and deployment.
Testing incoherent: package.json declares 'no test specified' despite test_b*.ts and __tests__ existing; no standardized framework (jest/vitest); scripts/ uses ad-hoc pass/fail pattern.
Observability failures: kill_switch.ts silently nulls proc on PowerShell spawn error; hermes_watcher.ts logs last_check_iso even on API failures, masking outages.
License ambiguity (ISC vs MIT) + missing documentation (docs/ unreadable) create unclear IP and onboarding barriers.
recommendations: Audit runner.ts immediately: wrap all execSync() calls with permissions.isDangerousCommand() checks; add unit tests verifying denied commands are blocked. File: src/utils/runner.ts.
Decouple hardcoded paths: replace C:\Users\angel\... with environment variables (SHINOBI_HOME) or path.resolve(process.env.USERPROFILE, '.shinobi'). File: scripts/a4_run.ts.
Migrate test suite: consolidate test_b*.ts and __tests__/ under a single framework (vitest recommended for TypeScript speed); update package.json test script to run framework CLI, not 'echo'.
Improve error semantics: make kill_switch.ts throw on spawn failure (don't silent-null proc); add explicit error logging to hermes_watcher.ts on API fails (separate from last_check_iso updates).
Establish doc accessibility: verify missions/, docs/, misc/ are readable (check file encoding, size, subagent limits); if >100MB, split or archive; document mission schema in repo root README.
Resolve license: pick ISC or MIT (recommend MIT for community); update package.json and add LICENSE file; document choice in CONTRIBUTING.md.

consensus.topic: runner.ts execSync bypasses permissions.isDangerousCommand(), enabling arbitrary command execution
Local-first architecture with optional cloud backend is a strength
Hardcoded Windows paths in scripts/a4_run.ts break portability
kill_switch.ts silently nulls proc on spawn failure, defeating the safety mechanism
hermes_watcher.ts updates last_check_iso on API failures, masking outages
Testing is incoherent: no standardized framework, npm test is a no-op despite test files existing
permissions.ts WORKSPACE_ROOT cwd() fallback weakens path-traversal protection
License ambiguity (ISC vs MIT) in package.json
Modular monorepo separation and pragmatic tech choices (better-sqlite3 + sqlite-vec) are strengths

combined_recommendations: Wire permissions.isDangerousCommand() into runner.ts execSync wrapper; block risky patterns (cmd.exe /c, powershell -Command) with logged denials and unit tests asserting blocks
Adopt vitest as the standardized test framework; consolidate test_b*.ts and __tests__/ under it and wire npm test to the CLI for CI enforcement
Replace hardcoded Windows paths in scripts/a4_run.ts with env vars (e.g., SHINOBI_HOME, USERPROFILE) or path.resolve relative to repo root; validate against WORKSPACE_ROOT
Modify kill_switch.ts to throw or emit a CRITICAL structured event on PowerShell spawn failure; never silently disable the safety mechanism
Fix hermes_watcher.ts to only persist last_check_iso on successful API responses; emit structured error logs on failure
Pin WORKSPACE_ROOT at module load from a required env var; assert at startup to prevent cwd drift
Resolve license ambiguity: pick ISC or MIT, update package.json, and add a LICENSE file
Audit missions/, docs/, misc/ directories (currently unreadable); manually review for unsafe code and document mission schema in README
Add a portability lint check to prevent reintroduction of hardcoded absolute paths
